skills.name: Variables
skills.desc: Variables are used to store data values in JavaScript. They are declared using 'var', 'let', or 'const' and can hold various data types such as numbers, strings, and objects.

skills.name: Data Types
skills.desc: Data types in JavaScript include numbers, strings, booleans, objects, arrays, and more. Understanding data types is crucial for effective programming.

skills.name: Arithmetic Operators
skills.desc: Arithmetic operators in JavaScript, like +, -, *, /, and %, are used for performing mathematical calculations.

skills.name: Comparison Operators
skills.desc: Comparison operators such as ==, ===, !=, >, <, >=, and <= are used to compare values in JavaScript and return boolean results.

skills.name: Logical Operators
skills.desc: Logical operators like && (AND), || (OR), and ! (NOT) are used for combining and negating boolean values in conditional expressions.

skills.name: Conditional Statements
skills.desc: Conditional statements, including 'if', 'else if' (elif), and 'else', allow you to execute different blocks of code based on specific conditions.

skills.name: Switch Statements
skills.desc: Switch statements provide a way to select one of many code blocks to be executed based on the value of an expression.

skills.name: Loops
skills.desc: Loops in JavaScript, such as 'for', 'while', and 'do...while', enable repetitive execution of code until a certain condition is met.

skills.name: Functions
skills.desc: Functions are reusable blocks of code that can be defined, called, and passed arguments in JavaScript, allowing for modular programming.

skills.name: String Manipulation
skills.desc: String manipulation involves tasks like concatenation, splitting, and extracting substrings from strings, which are essential for text processing.

skills.name: Array Methods
skills.desc: JavaScript provides numerous built-in array methods for manipulating arrays, including 'push', 'pop', 'map', 'filter', and 'reduce'.

skills.name: Object Manipulation
skills.desc: Object manipulation involves creating, modifying, and accessing object properties, which is fundamental to working with data structures.

skills.name: Object-Oriented Programming
skills.desc: Object-oriented programming (OOP) principles like encapsulation, inheritance, and polymorphism are used to create and manage objects in JavaScript.

skills.name: Asynchronous Programming
skills.desc: Asynchronous programming techniques like callbacks, promises, and async/await are employed to manage non-blocking operations, such as API requests.

skills.name: Error Handling
skills.desc: Error handling involves using try...catch blocks to handle exceptions and gracefully manage unexpected issues in JavaScript code.

skills.name: Closures
skills.desc: Closures are functions that capture and maintain their surrounding scope's variables, allowing for data encapsulation and privacy.

skills.name: Higher-Order Functions
skills.desc: Higher-order functions are functions that can take other functions as arguments or return them, facilitating functional programming.

skills.name: Prototypes and Inheritance
skills.desc: JavaScript uses prototypes for object inheritance, allowing objects to inherit properties and methods from other objects.

skills.name: Destructuring Assignment
skills.desc: Destructuring assignment provides a concise way to extract values from objects and arrays and assign them to variables.

skills.name: Template Literals
skills.desc: Template literals, introduced in ES6, allow for more flexible string interpolation and multiline strings in JavaScript.

skills.name: ES6 Features
skills.desc: ES6 (ECMAScript 2015) introduced various features like arrow functions, let/const declarations, and classes to enhance JavaScript development.

skills.name: DOM Selection
skills.desc: DOM selection involves selecting HTML elements from a web page using methods like getElementById, querySelector, and getElementsByClassName.

skills.name: DOM Manipulation
skills.desc: DOM manipulation allows you to modify the content, structure, and style of web pages dynamically using JavaScript.

skills.name: Event Handling
skills.desc: Event handling is essential for responding to user interactions like clicks and keypresses, enabling interactive web applications.

skills.name: DOM Traversal
skills.desc: DOM traversal techniques like traversing parent, child, and sibling elements are used to navigate and manipulate the DOM tree.

skills.name: Debugging
skills.desc: Debugging tools and techniques help identify and fix errors and issues in JavaScript code for smoother development.

skills.name: Browser Object Model
skills.desc: The Browser Object Model (BOM) provides access to browser-specific functionalities and properties, allowing interaction with the browser.

skills.name: Web Storage
skills.desc: Web storage mechanisms like localStorage and sessionStorage enable client-side storage of data for web applications.

skills.name: Version Control
skills.desc: Version control systems like Git are used for tracking and managing changes to JavaScript code, enabling collaboration and code history.

skills.name: HTTP Requests
skills.desc: JavaScript can make HTTP requests to fetch data from servers, allowing web applications to interact with external resources.

skills.name: JSON
skills.desc: JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) is a widely used format for data exchange, and JavaScript provides built-in support for parsing and generating JSON data.

skills.name: API Interaction
skills.desc: Interacting with APIs allows web applications to retrieve and send data to external services, enhancing functionality and data integration.

skills.name: Cross-Site Scripting
skills.desc: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) is a security vulnerability that JavaScript developers need to address to prevent unauthorized code execution on web pages.

skills.name: Web Security Basics
skills.desc: Understanding web security basics is essential for safeguarding web applications from common security threats and vulnerabilities.
